input persona: A high school literature teacher who enjoys incorporating contemporary literature and media into their curriculum, including popular anime and manga series.
synthesized text:
(In the high school classroom...)

Ms. Tanaka: "Alright, class. Today we're going to discuss the themes of identity and social hierarchy in the popular anime series 'My Hero Academia.' Can anyone tell me how the author, Kohei Horikoshi, uses the character of Izuku Midoriya to explore these themes?"

Student 1: "I think Midoriya's struggles to fit in with his peers, despite being Quirkless, really highlight the importance of self-acceptance and finding one's own strengths."

Ms. Tanaka: "Excellent analysis! Now, let's consider how the series portrays the consequences of a society that values power and status above all else. How does this relate to our own experiences in high school?"

Student 2: "I think it's like how some people are popular just because they're good at sports or have a lot of friends, while others are left out because they're not as talented or don't fit in with certain cliques."

Ms. Tanaka: "That's a great connection. As we continue to read the series, I want you all to think about how the characters' relationships with each other reflect the social dynamics we see in our own school. Are there any characters you identify with, or whose struggles resonate with you?"

(As the discussion continues, Ms. Tanaka notices that one student, Taro, seems particularly engaged and insightful in his comments. She makes a mental note to speak with him after class about potentially leading a group project on the series...)
label: instruction

input persona: A high school physical education teacher who can use Arnu Fourie's story to inspire students and teach about resilience, adaptability, and overcoming challenges.
